# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';
import type { NextRequest } from 'next/server';

// Define your protected routes that require authentication
const PROTECTED_ROUTES = ['/dashboard', '/profile', '/settings'];

// Define your authentication routes (e.g., login, register)
// If an authenticated user tries to access these, they will be redirected.
const AUTH_ROUTES = ['/login', '/register'];

export function middleware(request: NextRequest) {
  const { pathname } = request.nextUrl;

  // 1. Simulate authentication status
  // In a real application, you would securely verify the auth_token
  // (e.g., decode and validate a JWT, check against a session store).
  // For this example, we'll simply check if the token exists.
  const authToken = request.cookies.get('auth_token')?.value;
  const isAuthenticated = !!authToken; // True if token exists and is not empty

  // 2. Handle authenticated users
  if (isAuthenticated) {
    // If an authenticated user tries to access an authentication route (e.g., /login),
    // redirect them to a dashboard or home page.
    if (AUTH_ROUTES.some(route => pathname.startsWith(route))) {
      return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/dashboard', request.url));
    }
    // For all other routes, let authenticated users proceed.
    return NextResponse.next();
  }

  // 3. Handle unauthenticated users
  // If an unauthenticated user tries to access a protected route,
  // redirect them to the login page.
  if (PROTECTED_ROUTES.some(route => pathname.startsWith(route))) {
    const loginUrl = new URL('/login', request.url);
    // Optionally, you can add a query parameter to redirect back after successful login
    // loginUrl.searchParams.set('redirect_to', pathname);
    return NextResponse.redirect(loginUrl);
  }

  // 4. For all other cases (unauthenticated user accessing public routes or auth routes),
  // let them proceed.
  return NextResponse.next();
}

// Configure the matcher to specify which paths the middleware should run on.
// This helps optimize performance by only running the middleware where necessary.
export const config = {
  matcher: [
    /*
     * Match all request paths except for the ones starting with:
     * - /api (API routes)
     * - /_next/static (Next.js static assets)
     * - /_next/image (Next.js image optimization files)
     * - /favicon.ico (favicon file)
     * - Any file with a common static asset extension (e.g., .svg, .png, .jpg, .jpeg, .gif, .webp)
     */
    '/((?!api|_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|.*\\.(?:svg|png|jpg|jpeg|gif|webp)$).*)',
  ],
};
